query_normalized: the impact of bounded confidence on opinion polarization speed psychology
rationale: The paper investigates opinion polarization in autonomous AI agents within echo chambers, which is an off-domain application (AI agents vs. human psychology) and does not measure the specific "bounded confidence" mechanism or human opinion dynamics requested. While it shares the general topic of polarization, it fails to satisfy the criteria for same-mechanism evidence, empirical population, or foundational methodology for the specific psychological construct of bounded confidence in humans.
